introduction: A row of iron cages lines the wall, their doors hanging open. Inside, you see the aftermath of the cult's experiments.
Empty shackles. Dried blood on the stone floors. Scratches on the walls where prisoners clawed in desperation.
One cell contains a body - a villager who died before the cult could complete their transformation. Their face is frozen in an expression of terror.
You approach the body carefully. It's a young man, perhaps twenty years old. You recognize him from wanted posters in Redstone - one of the missing persons.
His skin has an unnatural gray pallor, and dark veins spiderweb across his exposed arms. The shadow blight started but didn't complete - he died during the transformation.
In his clenched fist, you find a scrap of paper with hastily scribbled words:

'They put something in our water. Made us see things. Hear voices. Some of us resisted. Those who gave in... changed. Became empty. If you find this, tell my family I tried to fight it. Tell them I'm sorry.'
The scratches on the walls form desperate messages:
'Day 8 - Can't remember my daughter's face anymore'
'Day 12 - They took Marcus today. He came back... different. Wrong.'
'Day 15 - I can feel it in my head. It promises peace. Part of me wants to give in.'
'Day 17 - Sarah gave in. Her eyes went black. She smiled at me. That smile was the worst part.'
The final message, scratched so deep it drew blood:
You search each cell methodically, hoping against hope to find someone still alive, someone you can save.
But there's no one. The cells are empty except for the one body. The rest became the possessed villagers you fought earlier - or worse, willing converts to the cult.
You find abandoned belongings: a child's doll, a wedding ring, a letter never sent. Each one a life destroyed by Marcus's ambition.
The weight of it settles on your shoulders. You're too late to save these people. But you can still stop this from happening to anyone else.

options.text: [Take the belongings to return to their families]
